mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market strength, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is always active because payrolls grow in most expansionary periods.
failure_condition: If payrolls growth decelerates and forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances, or the rank IC is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises the federal funds rate above 0.25%, it tightens financial conditions, increases discount rates, and reduces equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a rate hike above 0.25%, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and housing starts signals robust economic expansion that boosts corporate profits and stock prices over the next 20 trading days. The joint behavior creates a stronger signal than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rising over the last 3 months
failure_condition: If one-month forward returns are negative when both proxies are rising in more than 40% of instances, or the correlation of the interaction signal with returns is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth in a late-cycle expansion signals that consumer spending is overheating, which builds inflation pressures and forces the Fed to tighten, thereby depressing equity returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism is regime-conditional: positive in early cycle, negative in late cycle.
failure_condition: If when RSAFS 6-month growth exceeds 5% during late-cycle periods, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in the unemployment rate below the natural rate signals a tightening labor market that fuels wage inflation and anticipated Fed tightening, causing negative stock returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE is below 5%, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than half of the cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth consistently reflects real economic activity and capacity utilization, which drives corporate profits and equity prices higher over a 60-trading-day horizon. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is negative over any 5-year rolling period, or rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises above 2% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, monetary policy is accommodative despite inflation, which reduces real discount rates and boosts equities through both pricing power and low borrowing costs over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in this regime forward 20-day returns are negative more often than positive, or the interaction signal's predictive accuracy is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: GDP growth in a late-cycle expansion often exceeds potential, triggering monetary tightening that reduces equity valuations. Thus, above-trend GDP growth in this regime predicts negative 60-trading-day returns.
failure_condition: If when GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized in late-cycle conditions, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward.
